title: FitED: A User-Centric, Extensible Software Environment for Robust Peak-Profile and General Functional Data Fitting
abstract: Reliable parameter extraction from experimental data is central to quantitative analysis in spectroscopy, diffraction, photoluminescence, chromatography, microscopy, and time-resolved measurements. We present FitED, a Python-based desktop application for interactive and automated nonlinear fitting of one-dimensional scientific data. FitED combines an accessible graphical workflow with a numerical backend capable of fitting both conventional peak profiles and arbitrary user-defined analytical functions. The software supports Gaussian, Lorentzian, Pseudo-Voigt, and exact area-normalized Voigt profiles, together with custom functions such as exponential decays, stretched exponentials, saturation curves, and spectroscopy-specific response functions. It integrates robust text-file import, region-of-interest selection, background modeling, parameter bounds, weighting strategies, automated pre-fit search, iterative peak refinement, residual visualization, session persistence, and structured export of fitted curves, components, reports, and metadata. By combining mathematical transparency with practical usability, FitED aims to make nonlinear fitting more reproducible and accessible while preserving the parameter-level control required by experienced experimental researchers.
summary: FitED is a new Python-based tool that simplifies the process of fitting complex peaks and custom functions to experimental data. It combines the ease of a graphical interface with the flexibility of the scientific Python ecosystem, ensuring that data analysis is both accessible and reproducible.
main_problem: The difficulty of achieving reliable, reproducible, and accessible parameter extraction from complex 1D experimental data, specifically bridging the gap between overly restrictive GUI tools and complex, manual coding environments.
main_result: The development of FitED, a Python-based desktop application that provides a user-centric, extensible environment for both automated and interactive nonlinear fitting of peak profiles and custom analytical functions.
method: A layered architecture using a numerical backend (NumPy, SciPy, lmfit) and a graphical interface, implementing weighted nonlinear least-squares minimization and an AST parser for custom mathematical expressions.
model_or_system: The software is designed for 1D scientific data from platforms such as spectroscopy, diffraction, and time-resolved measurements, supporting models like Gaussian, Lorentzian, Pseudo-Voigt, and exact Voigt profiles, as well as custom decay and saturation curves.
key_observables: Peak centers, integrated areas, FWHM, Gaussian/Lorentzian widths, residuals, and chi-square values.
important_parameters: Parameter bounds, weighting strategies (unweighted, Poisson-like, etc.), and mixing fractions for Pseudo-Voigt profiles.
main_assumptions: The software is currently limited to one-dimensional, two-column data and assumes that custom backgrounds remain well-defined over the fitting interval.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the software architecture and the complete analysis pipeline from data import and preprocessing to optimization and final export.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the scientific problem, details the software architecture and numerical backend, describes the mathematical models and functional capabilities, explains the user workflow, and discusses limitations and future development.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: Software architecture and analysis workflow of FitED. Experimental data are imported, validated, cropped to a selected region of interest, and passed to the model-construction and optimization engine. The fitting engine combines built-in peak profiles, custom analytical expressions, background models, parameter constraints, and weighting strategies to produce reproducible fitted curves, residuals, component contributions, fit reports, and exportable session files.
